 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.7","title":"Implement wizard Step 4: Rent a VPS","description":"# Task: Implement Wizard Step 4 - Rent a VPS

## Description
Guide users to rent a VPS from a supported provider with appropriate specs.

## Page Copy
**Title:** Rent a VPS (~$50/month sweet spot)
**Body:**
Pick a VPS provider and rent a server. This is where your coding agents will live.

### Recommended Providers
- **OVH** - European, good value
- **Contabo** - Budget-friendly, high specs
- **Hetzner** - Developer favorite
- **Other** - Any provider with Ubuntu VPS works

### What to choose
- **OS:** Ubuntu 25.x (or newest Ubuntu available)
- **CPU:** 4-8 vCPU
- **RAM:** 8-16 GB
- **Storage:** 100GB+ NVMe SSD
- **Price:** ~$50/month is the sweet spot

### Important
- Make sure "SSH key login" is available
- You'll paste your public key in the next step

## Technical Details
- Provider cards that expand with specific guidance
- Checklist of what to select
- Links to signup pages

## Components
- ProviderChooser with expandable cards
- Checklist component

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Provider cards with logos
- [ ] Expandable provider-specific guidance
- [ ] Clear spec recommendations
- [ ] Links to provider signup
- [ ] "I rented a VPS" advances","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:23:33.751033-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:52:16.561453-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:52:16.561453-05:00","close_reason":"Closed","labels":["task","website","wizard-step"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.7","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:23:33.75301-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.8","title":"Implement wizard Steps 5-7: Create VPS, SSH In, Paste Installer","description":"# Task: Implement Wizard Steps 5-7

## Description
Implement the core VPS setup steps:
- Step 5: Create VPS and attach SSH key
- Step 6: SSH into your VPS
- Step 7: Paste the ACFS one-liner

## Step 5: Create VPS + Attach SSH Key
**Title:** Create your VPS and add your SSH key
**Content:**
1. Select Ubuntu 25.x as the OS image
2. Find "SSH Keys" section
3. Paste your public key (the one you copied earlier)
4. Create the server
5. Copy the IP address shown

Checklist:
- [ ] Selected Ubuntu image
- [ ] Pasted my SSH public key
- [ ] Server created
- [ ] I have the IP address

## Step 6: SSH In
**Title:** Connect to your VPS
**Command (Mac):**
```bash
ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new -i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519 ubuntu@YOUR_IP
```

**Command (Windows):**
```powershell
ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new -i $HOME\.ssh\acfs_ed25519 ubuntu@YOUR_IP
```

**Note:** If `ubuntu@` fails, try `root@` instead.

## Step 7: Paste the ACFS One-Liner
**Title:** Paste the magic command
**Command:**
```bash
curl -fsSL "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/main/install.sh?$(date +%s)" | bash -s -- --yes --mode vibe
```

**What this will do:**
- Create/enforce ubuntu user with passwordless sudo
- Install your full toolchain (shell, languages, agents)
- Install the Dicklesworthstone stack (all 8 tools)
- End by telling you what to type next

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Step 5: Provider-agnostic guidance
- [ ] Step 6: IP input field to customize command
- [ ] Step 7: Big, prominent command card
- [ ] Troubleshooting sections for common issues","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:23:48.826787-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:36:40.547518-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T22:36:40.547521-05:00","labels":["task","website","wizard-step"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.8","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:23:48.828541-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.9","title":"Implement wizard Steps 8-10: Reconnect, Status, Onboard","description":"# Task: Implement Wizard Steps 8-10

## Description
Complete the final wizard steps:
- Step 8: Reconnect as ubuntu
- Step 9: ACFS Status Check
- Step 10: Run onboard

## Step 8: Reconnect as Ubuntu
**Title:** Reconnect as ubuntu
**Body:**
If you installed while logged in as `root`, reconnect as `ubuntu`:
```bash
exit
ssh -i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519 ubuntu@YOUR_IP
```

**Skip if:** Already logged in as ubuntu

## Step 9: ACFS Status Check
**Title:** Verify your VPS is ready (one command)
**Body:**
Run this on your VPS:
```bash
acfs doctor
```

**What you should see:**
A checklist with lots of `PASS ✅` lines.

**Interactive checklist** - shows all installed tools:
- Identity & permissions
- Shell setup
- Core tools
- Coding agents
- Cloud/DB tools
- Dicklesworthstone stack

**Optional:** Paste JSON output for auto-check

## Step 10: Run Onboard + Start Vibe Coding
**Title:** You're ready! Start the tutorial
**Command:**
```bash
onboard
```

**Quick reference:**
- `cc` - Claude Code
- `cod` - Codex CLI
- `gmi` - Gemini CLI
- `ntm` - Named Tmux Manager
- `cass` - Session search
- `cm` - Memory system
- `ubs` - Bug scanner
- `bv` - Beads viewer

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Step 8: Skip option for non-root users
- [ ] Step 9: Full status checklist display
- [ ] Step 9: Optional JSON paste auto-check
- [ ] Step 10: Celebration UI (confetti?)
- [ ] Step 10: Quick reference card","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:04.4838-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:36:41.482071-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T22:36:41.482073-05:00","labels":["task","website","wizard-step"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.9","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:04.48597-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yh5","title":"run_as_user: make curl|bash pipelines fail fast","description":"Several scripts/lib/* helpers run commands as TARGET_USER via `bash -c "$cmd"` (agents.sh, languages.sh, cli_tools.sh, cloud_db.sh, stack.sh). That new bash does not inherit pipefail, so `curl ... | bash` can return success when curl fails (bash runs empty input). Prefix the spawned shell with `set -euo pipefail;` so pipeline failures propagate correctly.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FuchsiaCreek","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:21:53.334333-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T17:28:45.821485-05:00"}
+{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yh5","title":"run_as_user: make curl|bash pipelines fail fast","description":"Several scripts/lib/* helpers run commands as TARGET_USER via `bash -c "$cmd"` (agents.sh, languages.sh, cli_tools.sh, cloud_db.sh, stack.sh). That new bash does not inherit pipefail, so `curl ... | bash` can return success when curl fails (bash runs empty input). Prefix the spawned shell with `set -o pipefail;` so pipeline failures propagate correctly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FuchsiaCreek","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:21:53.334333-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T17:35:11.544723-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T17:35:11.544723-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented on main in commit 0de3e7330b2d133789f85167c063c0c9bee7cd52 (adds set -o pipefail to run-as-user helpers)."}
